title: Memory Addressing: LDR and STR
summary: Access memory in ARM using Load (LDR) and Store (STR) instructions with various addressing modes.
content_html:
ARM uses a load/store architecture — all memory access must go through LDR (load) and STR (store) instructions. Arithmetic operates only on registers.

LDR — Load Register

LDR R0, [R1]         ; R0 = Memory[R1]
LDR R0, [R1, #4]     ; R0 = Memory[R1 + 4] (offset)
LDR R0, [R1, #4]!    ; R0 = Memory[R1+4], R1 = R1+4 (pre-index)
LDR R0, [R1], #4     ; R0 = Memory[R1], R1 = R1+4 (post-index)

STR — Store Register

STR R0, [R1]         ; Memory[R1] = R0
STR R0, [R1, #8]     ; Memory[R1+8] = R0

Addressing Modes

  • Register indirect: [R1] — address is in R1
  • Offset: [R1, #4] — address is R1+4, R1 unchanged
  • Pre-indexed: [R1, #4]! — R1 updated to R1+4 before access
  • Post-indexed: [R1], #4 — access at R1, then R1 updated

Loading Constants

MOV R0, #255         ; Small constants direct
LDR R0, =0x12345678  ; Large constants via literal pool

exercises.title: LDR function
options: Loads the value at Memory[R1] into R0
Stores R0 into memory at address R1
Loads the address R1 into R0
exercises.answer: Loads the value at Memory[R1] into R0
